title: Chrysanthemum kinokuniense
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum kinokuniense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the subgenus Chrysanthemum within the genus Chrysanthemum. The subgenus Chrysanthemum is further divided into several sections, and Chrysanthemum kinokuniense falls under the section Indica. However, it is worth noting that some sources may classify the Chrysanthemum genus differently, as it is sometimes placed within the subfamily Anthemidinae. The exact classification can vary depending on the source and any recent taxonomic revisions.
uses: Chrysanthemum kinokuniense has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for various purposes. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat conditions such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections. The plant's leaves and flowers are typically used in herbal remedies to promote digestion and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In addition, Chrysanthemum kinokuniense is thought to have antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help to reduce fever, relieve pain, and boost the immune system. The plant contains flavonoids and saponins, which are known to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. intro: Chrysanthemum kinokuniense, also known as Kinokuni chrysanthemum, is a flowering plant species native to Japan, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Eastern medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, headache, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum kinokuniense include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
